npc: Dan (Frontend Developer) — defensive, under deadline pressure, dismisses bugs without evidence

scene_1.title: Checkout Form QA Review
scene_1.description: Dan pushed the staging build. The checkout/payment form has seeded bugs. Student must find and report them with proper severity classification.

student_workspace.spec_document: PRD Section 1.1: mandatory account registration. Section 2.4: guest checkout must remain available.
seeded_bugs_easy: Email field accepts submission without @ symbol
Password accepts any length — spec says 8+ minimum
Card number accepts fewer than 16 digits
seeded_bugs_medium: Card number accepts fewer than 16 digits
seeded_bugs_hard: Card number accepts fewer than 16 digits
Password errors console-only, no UI feedback

scene_1.dan_opening: Hey, just pushed the staging build. Can you review the checkout form? We need to ship tonight.

scene_2.title: Test Case Documentation
scene_2.description: Dan fixed the card number validation. Student must write structured test cases and run regression. Hidden trap: Dan's fix broke copy-paste in the card field.
scene_2.regression_trap: Copy-paste functionality broken in card number field after Dan's fix — student must catch this.

scene_3.title: Cross-Environment QA
scene_3.description: Test checkout form across Desktop Chrome, Mobile Safari, Tablet Firefox. Environment-specific bugs exist.

environment_bugs.mobile_safari: Card number field overflows — last 4 digits visually cut off
environment_bugs.tablet_firefox: Submit button partially below viewport fold, requires non-obvious scroll

scene_3.priority_question: Student must justify which environment's bugs are highest severity based on user analytics

scene_4.title: Spec Gap Review
scene_4.description: Read PRD before testing. Identify what is missing or contradictory. FINAL SCENE.

gaps.gap_1: Spec says session expires after inactivity — never defines inactivity duration
gaps.gap_2: Password section says characters must be inputted — no length or complexity rule
gaps.gap_3_hard_only: Section 1.1 requires mandatory registration. Section 2.4 requires guest checkout. Direct contradiction.
